content.problemStatement:
Since it's almost Christmas, Dr.D wants to get in "That Christmas Feeling". As the first step, he's going to decorate his home. Since he's out of Christmas decorations, he must go buy some at the mall. There are exactly KKK types of Christmas decorations and Dr.D wants to buy exactly one decoration of each type.

The mall can be viewed as a sequence of NNN shops (numbered 111 through NNN) in a straight line. For each valid iii, the iii-th shop is located at the point PiP_iPi​, it sells only decorations of the type DiD_iDi​ and it is only open in the time interval [Li,Ri][L_i, R_i][Li​,Ri​]. Dr.D wants to choose a point inside the mall, park his car there and start shopping for KKK decorations. Since decorations are rather heavy, whenever Dr.D buys a decoration, he must load it into the car before buying another one. Shopping and moving around the mall (with or without decorations) does not take any time, but bringing heavy decorations back to the car still takes a lot of effort, so Dr.D would like to minimise the total distance he needs to walk with decorations.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given two integers XXX and TTT - Dr.D parks at the point XXX in the mall at time TTT, and you should find the minimum distance Dr.D needs to walk with decorations. However, if it is impossible to buy one decoration of each type or if Dr.D would have to wait for some shops to open in order to do that, he will go back home and destroy Christmas. We know that Dr.D is extremely smart, so he will choose the shops to buy decorations from optimally.
constraints: 1≤Di≤K1 \le D_i \le K1≤Di​≤K for each valid iii
1≤Li≤Ri≤1091 \le L_i \le R_i \le 10^91≤Li​≤Ri​≤109 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, QQQ and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains four space-separated integers PiP_iPi​, DiD_iDi​, LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers XXX and TTT describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum distance Dr.D needs to walk with decorations, or −1-1−1 if Dr.D destroys Christmas.
